verdict: The AI displacement risk for this occupation is moderate, with a score of 65 indicating that while some tasks are automatable, the role requires human judgment and relationship-building skills.

reasoning_steps.title: High routine_cognitive score
reasoning_steps.body: The occupation involves tasks like monitoring markets, keeping accurate records of transactions, and reporting positions or trading results, which are all pattern-matching on text/data that current frontier LLMs can handle.

reasoning_steps.title: Moderate creative_judgment score
reasoning_steps.body: While some tasks involve analyzing clients' financial status and developing financial plans, the role also requires human judgment and relationship-building skills to advise customers about stocks, bonds, and market conditions.

reasoning_steps.title: Low regulatory_moat score
reasoning_steps.body: The occupation does not require licensure or fiduciary duty, which means that AI can potentially perform some tasks in this role with minimal human oversight.

reasoning_steps.title: High stakes_of_error score
reasoning_steps.body: The occupation involves significant consequences of errors, such as financial losses for clients, which requires human judgment and expertise to mitigate risks.

reasoning_steps.title: Moderate relationships score
reasoning_steps.body: The occupation requires building and maintaining relationships with clients to advise them about financial options and keep them informed about transactions.

reasoning_steps.title: High ai_capability_gap score
reasoning_steps.body: Current frontier models can already perform many tasks in this occupation, such as monitoring markets and reporting positions, which reduces the need for human intervention.

adjacent_careers.name: Financial Advisor
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both roles involve advising clients on financial options, but financial advisors have more complex tasks like investment planning and portfolio management.

adjacent_careers.name: Investment Banker
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both roles involve high-stakes decision-making and require human judgment, but investment bankers have more complex tasks like deal-making and risk management.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered trading platform launched
recent_developments.impact: The new platform uses machine learning to analyze market trends and make trades, potentially reducing the need for human intervention in trading.

recent_developments.headline: Banks roll out AI copilots to analysts and traders
recent_developments.impact: JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman now offer LLM tools to thousands of frontline staff.

quotes.quote: AI is going to change every job. Every single job. Some jobs it will eliminate, some jobs it will improve, and some jobs it will create.
quotes.speaker: Jamie Dimon
quotes.title: CEO, JPMorgan Chase

quotes.quote: Two-thirds of jobs in the United States and Europe are exposed to some degree of AI automation, and roughly a quarter of current work could be done by generative AI.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Briggs and Devesh Kodnani
quotes.title: Economists, Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs report: The Potentially Large Effects of AI on Economic Growth

quotes.quote: Generative AI could add the equivalent of 2.6 to 4.4 trillion dollars in value annually across the global economy. Banking, high-tech, and life sciences capture the largest share.
quotes.speaker: McKinsey Global Institute
quotes.title: Research report
quotes.source: The Economic Potential of Generative AI
